title: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil

seo.imageAlt: Freshly baked Rosemary Focaccia Genovese with dimples, coarse salt, and fresh rosemary sprigs on a wooden board.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the irresistible aroma and taste of homemade Rosemary Focaccia Genovese, a true Italian classic!

description: Imagine biting into a cloud-like bread, crispy on the outside and incredibly soft within, generously drizzled with Ligurian olive oil and fragrant rosemary. This authentic Focaccia Genovese is a true taste of Italy, perfect as an appetizer or side. Its simple ingredients combine to create a deeply satisfying experience.

ingredients.notes: plus extra for drizzling and pan

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the warm water and yeast. Let it sit for 5 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active. Stir in 2 tablespoons of olive oil.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: Add the flour and fine sea salt to the yeast mixture. Mix with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. Transfer the dough to a lightly oiled surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until it is smooth and elastic. The dough will be quite wet.

instructions.title: First Rise
instructions.text: Lightly oil a clean bowl, place the dough in it, and turn to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let it rise in a warm place for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Prepare for Baking
instructions.text: Generously oil a 9x13 inch baking pan with olive oil. Gently transfer the risen dough to the pan, stretching it carefully to fill the bottom. Drizzle with a little more olive oil.

instructions.title: Dimple and Second Rise
instructions.text: Using your fingertips, create deep dimples all over the dough. Sprinkle with chopped fresh rosemary and coarse sea salt. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and let it rise again for 30-45 minutes.

instructions.title: Bake the Focaccia
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 220C (425F). Bake the focaccia for 25-35 minutes, or until golden brown on top and cooked through. If the top browns too quickly, you can loosely cover it with foil.

instructions.title: Final Drizzle and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, immediately remove the focaccia from the oven. Drizzle generously with extra virgin olive oil. Let it cool slightly on a wire rack before slicing and serving warm. Buon appetito!

tips: For the best texture, use strong bread flour with a high protein content. This helps develop the gluten needed for a chewy focaccia.
Do not skimp on the olive oil. A generous amount of good quality extra virgin olive oil is crucial for the authentic flavor and crispy bottom crust of Genovese focaccia.
Serve your focaccia warm, ideally straight from the oven. If reheating, a quick toast in a pan or oven will revive its crispness.
article:
Few things encapsulate the soul of Italian baking quite like focaccia. And when we talk about focaccia, the Genovese style, particularly infused with fragrant rosemary and rich olive oil, stands in a league of its own. This isn't just bread; it's an experience – a golden, dimpled canvas of simple perfection, born from the sun-drenched Ligurian coast.

Imagine a bread that's simultaneously airy and substantial, with a crisp, salty crust that gives way to a soft, yielding interior. That’s the magic of Focaccia Genovese, a staple that has graced tables across Italy and beyond for centuries. It's the kind of bread that makes you close your eyes and savor every bite, especially when the pungent aroma of fresh rosemary mingles with the fruity notes of high-quality olive oil. Whether you're a seasoned baker or a curious beginner, mastering this Italian classic is a deeply rewarding endeavor that brings a taste of Liguria directly into your home kitchen.

## What is Rosemary Focaccia Genovese?
Originating from Genoa, the capital of Italy's Liguria region, Focaccia Genovese is renowned for its distinctive characteristics. Unlike its denser counterparts, this focaccia boasts an incredibly light, airy crumb with a crisp, golden crust. The hallmark of authentic Focaccia Genovese lies in its generous use of extra virgin olive oil, both within the dough and liberally drizzled over the top before baking, creating those iconic, flavorful dimples. The addition of fresh rosemary is not merely an afterthought; it’s integral to the aromatic profile, weaving its piney, citrusy notes throughout every bite. This specific combination – the superior olive oil, the delicate texture, and the fragrant rosemary – defines Rosemary Focaccia Genovese as a true culinary masterpiece. It's a testament to how a few high-quality ingredients, handled with care, can yield extraordinary results.

## Ingredients You'll Need for Genovese Focaccia
Crafting this exquisite bread requires a surprisingly simple list of ingredients, but their quality is paramount.
*   **Strong Bread Flour:** High protein content is essential for developing the gluten structure that gives focaccia its characteristic chew and airiness. All-purpose flour can work in a pinch, but bread flour is preferred.
*   **Active Dry or Instant Yeast:** The leavening agent that makes the dough rise. Ensure your yeast is fresh and active.
*   **Warm Water:** Activates the yeast and helps bring the dough together.
*   **Extra Virgin Olive Oil:** This is non-negotiable. Use the best quality you can afford. It contributes immensely to the flavor, texture, and signature crispness. You'll need it for the dough, for oiling the pan, and for drizzling.
*   **Fresh Rosemary:** Absolutely vital for that iconic aroma and flavor. Dried rosemary can be used as a last resort, but fresh sprigs are superior.
*   **Sea Salt:** Both for the dough and for sprinkling on top. Flaky sea salt adds a beautiful textural crunch and bursts of salinity on the crust.

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Perfect Focaccia
Embarking on the journey to bake your own Rosemary Focaccia Genovese is a rewarding experience. Follow these steps for a perfect loaf:
1.  **Activate the Yeast:** In a large bowl, combine warm water and yeast. Let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active.
2.  **Mix the Dough:** Add the flour, a generous drizzle of olive oil, and salt to the yeast mixture. Mix with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms.
3.  **Knead:** Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Knead for 8-10 minutes until it's smooth, elastic, and no longer sticky. Alternatively, use a stand mixer with a dough hook for 5-7 minutes.
4.  **First Proof (Bulk Fermentation):** Lightly grease a large bowl with olive oil. Place the dough in the bowl, turning to coat. Cover with plastic wrap or a damp towel and let it rise in a warm place for 1-1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.
5.  **Prepare the Pan:** Liberally coat a rectangular baking pan (approx. 9x13 inches) with extra virgin olive oil. This prevents sticking and contributes to the crispy bottom.
6.  **Second Proof & Dimpling:** Gently transfer the risen dough to the prepared pan. Using your fingertips, gently stretch the dough to fill the pan. Don't force it; if it springs back, let it rest for 10 minutes, then continue stretching. Once stretched, use your oiled fingertips to create deep dimples all over the surface of the dough.
7.  **Flavoring:** Drizzle more olive oil generously over the dimpled dough. Scatter fresh rosemary needles and a good sprinkle of flaky sea salt over the top.
8.  **Final Proof:** Cover the pan loosely with plastic wrap or a damp towel and let it rise again for 30-45 minutes, or until noticeably puffy.
9.  **Preheat Oven:** While the dough is proofing, preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C).
10. **Bake:** Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the focaccia is golden brown on top and cooked through. The bottom should also be golden and crisp.
11. **Finish:** Immediately upon removing from the oven, drizzle a final touch of high-quality extra virgin olive oil over the hot focaccia. This enhances the flavor and keeps it moist.

## Pro Tips for the Best Rosemary Focaccia
Achieving that sublime texture and flavor for your Rosemary Focaccia Genovese goes beyond just following the steps. Here are some insider tips:
*   **Don't Skimp on Olive Oil:** This is the soul of Genovese focaccia. Use it generously in the dough, for greasing the pan, for dimpling, and for the final drizzle. The quality truly matters here.
*   **Patience with Proofing:** Rushing the rise times will result in a dense focaccia. Let the yeast do its work in a warm, draft-free environment.
*   **Gentle Handling:** Once the dough has risen, handle it gently to preserve the air bubbles that contribute to its light texture.
*   **Deep Dimples:** Don't be shy with the dimples! They are crucial for creating pockets for the olive oil and salt, leading to those delicious crispy, chewy bits.
*   **Hot Oven:** A high oven temperature ensures a quick rise in the oven (oven spring) and a beautiful golden crust.
*   **Fresh Rosemary is Key:** While dried herbs can be used, fresh rosemary imparts a much more vibrant and authentic flavor.

## Serving Suggestions for Focaccia Genovese
The beauty of Focaccia Genovese is its versatility. It can be enjoyed in countless ways:
*   **As is:** Simply tear off a piece and savor its chewy interior and crispy, salty exterior. It’s perfect on its own as a snack or appetizer.
*   **With Dips:** Serve alongside a small bowl of your finest extra virgin olive oil for dipping, perhaps with a sprinkle of balsamic vinegar.
*   **Antipasto Platter:** A fantastic addition to any charcuterie or cheese board. Its light texture and savory flavor complement cured meats, olives, and various cheeses beautifully.
*   **Sandwich Base:** Slice it horizontally to make delicious sandwiches. Fill with prosciutto, mortadella, fresh mozzarella, roasted vegetables, or grilled chicken.
*   **Accompaniment:** It pairs wonderfully with soups, stews, or salads, soaking up flavorful broths and dressings.
*   **Breakfast/Brunch:** Believe it or not, a slice of focaccia with a fried egg or some avocado makes for a surprisingly delightful start to the day.

## Variations and Substitutions
While the classic Rosemary Focaccia Genovese is a masterpiece, feel free to experiment:
*   **Olives:** Add pitted Kalamata or green olives to the top before baking.
*   **Cherry Tomatoes:** Halved cherry tomatoes pressed into the dimples add a burst of sweetness and acidity.
*   **Onions:** Thinly sliced red onions can be caramelized slightly before baking on top.
*   **Other Herbs:** Thyme or oregano can be used in combination with or instead of rosemary, though the classic is hard to beat.
*   **Garlic:** Thinly sliced garlic cloves can be added with the rosemary for an extra aromatic kick.
*   **Cheese:** A sprinkle of Parmesan or Pecorino Romano can be added during the last few minutes of baking for a cheesy crust.
*   **Gluten-Free:** While challenging, gluten-free flour blends specifically designed for bread can be used, but the texture will differ significantly from traditional focaccia.

## Storage and Reheating Tips
Focaccia is best enjoyed fresh, ideally on the day it’s baked. However, if you have leftovers:
*   **Room Temperature:** Store cooled focaccia in an airtight container or wrapped tightly in foil for up to 2 days. The crust will soften, but the flavor will remain.
*   **Freezing:** For longer storage, slice the focaccia, wrap individual pieces tightly in plastic wrap, and then place them in a freezer-safe bag or container. It can be frozen for up to 1 month.
*   **Reheating:** To revive focaccia, especially from the freezer, a few minutes in a toaster oven or a regular oven preheated to 350°F (175°C) will bring back some of its crispness. You can also lightly grill it in a pan. Avoid the microwave, as it will make the focaccia chewy.

Baking your own Rosemary Focaccia Genovese is a journey into the heart of Italian culinary tradition. It's a simple recipe, yet one that yields incredible depth of flavor and texture, transforming humble ingredients into something truly extraordinary. Whether enjoyed on its own, as part of a lavish meal, or as the foundation for a gourmet sandwich, this iconic bread is sure to delight. So, roll up your sleeves, gather your ingredients, and prepare to fill your kitchen with the irresistible aroma of authentic Italian baking.
